And so the guy that springs for the Kobe Bryant has got himself attached to a new line of watches. They started like 60 Gs. So you can have a Kobe Bryant block mombo watch for $60,000. Is there $60,000 of intrinsic value? Well, no, the intrinsic value of a watch is what? Tells time. Actually, it doesn't. You have to look at it. Although they're selling one now. The guys that are selling the Jitterbug phone who are brilliant, these guys. They're selling a watch you don't have to look at. You set it for how often you want to tell you the time and it tells you the time. And it speaks, right? It tells you the time, which is really cool. But so the intrinsic value of a watch is that it tells time. That's it. What else is there? And maybe if you want to, you could argue that fashion is an intrinsic value. And so it looks good, but you don't need to spend 60 to even get to that. So whatever above that, what's the time next these days? Anybody know? 29 bucks. He just looked at his. So now we know who. Just stay away from the product table so you're not in a way of anybody who might spend some real money. If you see him coming, if we got a trainee, but so okay, so 29 bucks. I mean, a really nice fashionable quality. What's a tag these days? What's a tag you are? That's what I used to wear. What are those? 3, 400 bucks? I mean, certainly for a thousand bucks, we can get a really good watch that tells time and looks good. It doesn't turn our skin green, can't we? So the difference between the thousand and the 60,000, which is $59,000 of the 60,000, is IPU value. It's not intrinsic value, but value is value is value is value. And that's where we've got to get my favorite story about this. Some of you have heard it, but I'll tell it because I like it. And it is instructive. So the original Halbert's business was the family crest business, right? You got the letter that told you they'd researched the Gravett family crest back to... Where's Gravett from? What are you? French. French? Really? See, I would have guessed Bolshevik or German or something, so it's French. So they've researched the Gravett family crest back to France, and you can have a nice little drawing of it and information about it for $9.95, and then you buy that, and then they would sell you a nice big full color one to put up on your wall. Now, the first problem with all that is it was a Gravett family crest. It wasn't your Gravett family crest because clearly it couldn't be. So there's allusion there. But the cool part of the business that I really love that Gary's partner, Dennis Haslanger, invented was the Gravett family reunion tour. Because I don't know how they did this without a straight... Sold this with a straight face. You couldn't do it person, only on the phone. Because... So the Gravett family reunion tour, all the Gravettes, the Gravett customers who bought this stuff were invited on the Gravett family reunion tour to France, and they loaded them all up and they took them over there and they put them on tour buses. And they gave them the same tour anybody else would get except anything that was named Gravett. That's where they stopped at. So if there was a Gravett castle, a Gravett pub, a Gravett river, a Gravett rock, and if there weren't enough of them things, they stuck some signs up for the three days. And so everybody gets off the bus, stands in front of the Gravett pub, everybody takes a picture, everybody gets their ass back on the bus and goes on to the next deal. Now, so first of all, the whole thing is an illusion, right? But obviously all the Gravettes on the tour are not actually related. Somehow, maybe, maybe, maybe, maybe, but somehow, maybe, maybe, maybe, they're also related to a chimpanzee. So they're not getting on a bus and riding around with a bunch of chimps and going around and looking at Africa. So this makes about as much sense on a rational level. None of these people are really related. They just got the same last name, not necessarily the same. But they're all on the Gravett family reunion tour with other Gravettes. There's nobody there but Gravettes.
